lines.english: What kind of shopping does the writer enjoy, do you think?
lines.chinese: 你认为作者喜欢哪种购物方式？
lines.raw: What kind of shopping does the writer enjoy, do you think? | 你认为作者喜欢哪种购物方式？

lines.english: 'Do you call that a hat?' I said to my wife.
lines.chinese: “你管那玩意儿叫帽子？”我对妻子说。
lines.raw: 'Do you call that a hat?' I said to my wife. | “你管那玩意儿叫帽子？”我对妻子说。

lines.english: 'You needn't be so rude about it,'
lines.raw: 'You needn't be so rude about it,' | “你没必要这么粗鲁，”

lines.english: my wife answered as she looked at herself in the mirror.
lines.chinese: 妻子对着镜子看着自己，回答道。
lines.raw: my wife answered as she looked at herself in the mirror. | 妻子对着镜子看着自己，回答道。

lines.english: I sat down on one of those modern chairs with holes in it and waited.
lines.chinese: 我坐在一把带洞的现代椅子上，等着。
lines.raw: I sat down on one of those modern chairs with holes in it and waited. | 我坐在一把带洞的现代椅子上，等着。

lines.english: We had been in the hat shop for half an hour and my wife was still in front of the mirror.
lines.chinese: 我们已经在帽子店里待了半个小时了，妻子还在镜子前面。
lines.raw: We had been in the hat shop for half an hour and my wife was still in front of the mirror. | 我们已经在帽子店里待了半个小时了，妻子还在镜子前面。

lines.english: 'We mustn't buy things we don't need,' I remarked suddenly.
lines.chinese: “我们不该买不需要的东西，”我突然说道。
lines.raw: 'We mustn't buy things we don't need,' I remarked suddenly. | “我们不该买不需要的东西，”我突然说道。

lines.english: I regretted saying it almost at once.
lines.chinese: 我几乎立刻就后悔说了这句话。
lines.raw: I regretted saying it almost at once. | 我几乎立刻就后悔说了这句话。

lines.english: 'You needn't have said that,' my wife answered.
lines.chinese: “你本没必要说那些话的，”妻子回答道。
lines.raw: 'You needn't have said that,' my wife answered. | “你本没必要说那些话的，”妻子回答道。

lines.english: 'I needn't remind you of that terrible tie you bought yesterday.'
lines.chinese: “我也没必要提醒你昨天买的那条糟糕的领带。”
lines.raw: 'I needn't remind you of that terrible tie you bought yesterday.' | “我也没必要提醒你昨天买的那条糟糕的领带。”

lines.english: 'I find it beautiful,' I said. 'A man can never have too many ties.'
lines.chinese: “我觉得它很漂亮，”我说，“男人永远不嫌领带多。”
lines.raw: 'I find it beautiful,' I said. 'A man can never have too many ties.' | “我觉得它很漂亮，”我说，“男人永远不嫌领带多。”

lines.english: 'And a woman can't have too many hats,' she answered.
lines.chinese: “女人也永远不嫌帽子多，”她回答道。
lines.raw: 'And a woman can't have too many hats,' she answered. | “女人也永远不嫌帽子多，”她回答道。

lines.english: Ten minutes later we walked out of the shop together.
lines.chinese: 十分种后，我们一起走出了商店。
lines.raw: Ten minutes later we walked out of the shop together. | 十分种后，我们一起走出了商店。

lines.english: My wife was wearing a hat that looked like a lighthouse!
lines.chinese: 妻子戴着一顶看起来像灯塔一样的帽子！
lines.raw: My wife was wearing a hat that looked like a lighthouse! | 妻子戴着一顶看起来像灯塔一样的帽子！
